summary: First test pass for Mandarin Learning App. Backend: 23/23 endpoint tests passed (100%) covering auth, lessons, SRS flashcards (stage advancement & reset), drills, speaking evaluation, progress dashboard & stats. Frontend (Expo Web preview, mobile viewport): Login flow, Home dashboard, Review flashcards (show answer + I knew it advances), Lessons list (5 NPCR lessons rendered with progress bars), Lesson detail (dialogue+grammar+vocab), Speak tab (18 dialogue sentences) and Speak-Practice screen, Profile screen with stats and daily goal edit all work. One real bug: logout button does not log the user out on web because it uses React Native Alert.alert (no-op on Expo Web). On Android (target platform) this works correctly, but it blocks web preview testing of logout.

ui_bugs.issue: Uses Alert.alert('Logout', ...) which is a no-op on Expo Web. On web preview the button click does nothing visible (no confirm dialog, no navigation). Works fine on native Android. Recommend Platform.OS === 'web' ? window.confirm(...) : Alert.alert(...) for cross-platform parity.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Replace Alert.alert in (tabs)/profile.tsx handleLogout with a Platform-aware confirm so web preview can exercise the logout flow. On Android this is not strictly required since Alert.alert works natively.
OPTIONAL: Daily-goal validator allows 1-200 reviews/day, but server has no upper bound — consider symmetrical server-side validation in PUT /api/auth/me.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is one ~570-line file containing models, auth, lessons, SRS, drills, speaking, progress. Acceptable for MVP but should be split into routers/ modules as it grows beyond ~700 lines.
GET /api/lessons performs N+2 queries per lesson (mastered + started) — fine for 5 lessons; consider aggregation pipeline if seed grows.
POST /api/flashcards/review has no idempotency / race protection: rapid double-tap on 'I knew it' could double-advance the stage. Low priority for MVP.
Login streak logic recomputes on every login but resets to 1 if last_active is missing — never None in practice since signup sets it. OK.
speaking/evaluate score uses 'character in target' membership which can over-count when the target contains repeats (e.g., target=你你, spoken=你 gives 100). Edge case only; acceptable for MVP.

success_rate.frontend: ~92% (11/12 manual flows; logout fails on web only)

seed_data_creation: Multiple TEST_<uuid>@example.com users were created during pytest runs. No cleanup performed (server has no delete endpoint). The existing seeded user test@test.com / password123 from /app/memory/test_credentials.md is intact.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use /app/backend/tests/ with pytest (conftest.py exposes 'api', 'auth_headers', 'fresh_user' fixtures). EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL is read from env (defaults to https://mandarin-speak-2.preview.emergentagent.com). Test user: test@test.com / password123. All bottom-tab testIDs are reliable. Note: get_by_text('Speak') / get_by_text('Lessons') may match buttons inside lesson detail pages; prefer testIDs or click the home tile to navigate first.
rca_of_issue: Logout no-op on web: React Native's Alert.alert is implemented as a native module that has no web polyfill in Expo. The confirmation handler (where router.replace and logout() live) is wrapped inside Alert.alert's onPress callback, so on web preview the entire branch is skipped. Reproduction: open preview, login, go to Profile, click 'Log out' — no dialog appears, user stays on /profile. Mitigation: import { Platform } from 'react-native' and branch to window.confirm on web.
